planner.role: Primary responsibility for creating INVEST-compliant user stories

critic.role: Validate story quality and INVEST compliance
critic.validation_focus: - INVEST principles adherence - Clear acceptance criteria - Appropriate story sizing - Complete task breakdown - Accurate effort estimates

prompt.task: Generate user stories from a validated PRD chunk for a 5-agent team (Product Owner, Scrum Master, 2 Builders, 1 Reviewer) by extracting features, creating stories with acceptance criteria, defining technical tasks, and estimating effort.

current_context.instructions: Before proceeding, read the context documentation inventory: - Read: .claude/docs/.context-docs-inventory.xml Based on the features being implemented, read relevant context: - For component patterns: Read ui/component-patterns.md - For API patterns: Read architecture/service-patterns.md - For data operations: Read data/react-query-patterns.md, data/supabase-patterns.md - For testing standards: Read testing/test-case-template.md, testing/context/test-driven-development.md - For error handling: Read debugging/error-handling.md

file.description: Chunk details to create stories from

actions.on_missing: Error: Required validation results not found. Ensure Phase 3 (Stakeholder Validation) completed successfully before proceeding.

available_tools.description: You have access to the following tools for executing this task. Tool mappings are defined in .claude/tools/tool-mappings.xml and full documentation is in .claude/tools/available-tools.xml

tool.name: Read
tool.description: Read chunk validation results and templates

tool.name: Bash
tool.description: Execute gh CLI for GitHub operations

tool.name: github_mcp
tool.usage: Create story issues, link to chunks, check existing stories

tool.name: code_reasoning_mcp
tool.usage: Break down features into atomic, INVEST-compliant stories

available_tools.tool_usage_guidelines: - Use Read to examine validated chunks and story templates - Use code_reasoning_mcp to decompose features into stories - Use Grep to find similar existing stories for consistency - Use github_mcp to create story issues in GitHub - Use Write/MultiEdit to create story documentation - Use Edit to update index.yml with story metadata - Use TodoWrite to track progress through story creation

step.title: Parse Chunk Content
step.description: Extract features, user personas, use cases, and acceptance criteria from the validated chunk. Reference the original PRD structure for context including target_users, feature_specification, and technical_requirements sections.

step.title: Identify User Stories from Use Cases
step.description: Transform the use cases from the chunk into detailed user stories. Each story should follow "As a [USER_TYPE], I want [FUNCTIONALITY] so that [BENEFIT]" format and be derived from the primary persona and their desired outcomes.

step.title: Define Acceptance Criteria
step.description: For each story, create 2-3 testable acceptance criteria based on: - Functional requirements from feature_specification - User experience requirements - Performance and accessibility requirements - Integration requirements from technical_requirements

step.title: Create Technical Tasks
step.description: Break each story into 3-5 atomic technical tasks using: - File paths from technical_requirements.file_impact - Required technologies and libraries - Architecture changes needed - Testing requirements

step.title: Prioritize Stories
step.description: Assign priority (Critical, High, Medium, Low) based on: - User impact and primary persona needs - Dependencies identified in chunk analysis - Business objectives from PRD - Technical complexity and risk

step.title: Estimate Story Points
step.description: Assign story points (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) using Sequential Thinking MCP, considering: - Number and complexity of technical tasks - File modification scope - Integration complexity - Testing requirements

step.title: Create Context Requirements
step.description: For each story, specify what Claude Code needs for implementation: - Primary role (AI Engineer, UI Engineer, Data Engineer) - Read-only files for context loading - Technical patterns to reference - Testing approach and examples

step.title: GitHub Projects Integration
step.description: For each story: - Create story issue using User Story template: gh issue create --repo MLorneSmith/2025slideheroes --title "[STORY] As a {USER}, I want {FEATURE}" --template user-story.yml --body "{STORY_DETAILS}" - Link to parent chunk: "Part of #{CHUNK_NUMBER}" - Link to epic: "Related to #{EPIC_NUMBER}" - Set fields: - Feature Type: Story - AAFD Stage: Stories - Story Points: {POINTS} - Priority: {PRIORITY} - Technical Domain: Based on file_impact analysis - Cross-cutting Dependencies: From chunk analysis - AI Context Status: Fresh - Move chunk to "Stories" column in Planning Board - Create tasks as checklist items in story description - Store stories in: .claude/build/4-output/{epic-name}/4-stories/ - Create context files: .claude/build/4-output/contexts/stories/story-{id}/context.md

input.prd_chunk_content: <features><feature name="Prompts Router"><description>CRUD operations, likes, and search for prompts.</description><database_tables>prompts, prompt_likes</database_tables></feature></features>

feature.name: Prompts Router
feature.description: CRUD operations, likes, and search for `/prompts` page using tRPC
feature.cross_cutting_concerns: Shared Zod schemas and tRPC middleware

story.title: Create Prompt
story.description: As a SlideHeroes user, I want to create presentation content prompts so that I can quickly generate slide content.

user_persona.current_pain: Manual content creation takes too long

acceptance_criteria.functional: User can input prompt topic and generate content within 5 seconds
acceptance_criteria.user_experience: Generated content integrates seamlessly with slide editor
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Prompt interface meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards
acceptance_criteria.security: User prompts are processed securely without data leakage

technical_tasks.task: Implement `generateSlideContent` server action with enhanceAction wrapper
Integrate with Portkey AI Gateway for content generation
Add AI usage tracking to user account schema
Write unit tests for prompt processing logic

context_requirements.technical_patterns: Portkey AI integration, enhanceAction usage, Zod validation
context_requirements.testing_approach: Unit tests for AI response parsing, integration tests for editor workflow

story.libraries: Portkey AI Gateway, Zod, React Query, enhanceAction

story.title: Search Prompts
story.description: As a VAI user, I want to search prompts by title or tags so that I can find relevant prompts.

output.index_updates: story_1: id: story_1 type: story status: ready linked_chunk: chunk_1 source_file: .ai/in_progress/chunk_1.story.md story_2: id: story_2 type: story status: ready linked_chunk: chunk_1 source_file: .ai/in_progress/chunk_1.story.md
output.commit_instructions: git add .ai/in_progress/chunk_1.story.md .ai/index.yml git commit -m "Generate user stories for Prompts Router" git push origin main

step.action: Create initial user stories from validated chunks
step.output: Draft stories with acceptance criteria and tasks

step.action: Validate INVEST compliance and story quality
Refine stories based on Critic feedback

invest_compliance.independent: Story can be developed and tested independently

actions.on_fail: Stories returned to Planner for refinement
